content.question: Would Hungary's domestic political consensus on a structural reform to the 6/8-year track remain stable across a full implementation cycle (teacher redeployment, curriculum rewriting, 2–3 cohort years of transition), or does the Polish case suggest implementation disruption will erode political support regardless of long-run benefits?
content.reduction_path: Conduct structured interviews with Hungarian education stakeholders (teachers, parents, local authorities, opposition parties, academics) on their support for reform conditional on implementation timeline and consultation process. Examine the Polish 2015–2022 period for longitudinal opinion polling on the 2017 reform, correlating public support with phase of implementation. Use findings to design a phased intervention with built-in checkpoints and stakeholder review.
